title: Infosys clarifies DGGI show cause notice over alleged ineligible GST ITC refunds
detailed_summary: Infosys clarified media reports regarding a Directorate General of GST Intelligence show cause notice connected to GST refunds claimed by the Company. DGGI sought information in May 2025, issued a pre-show cause on 30 th  July 2025, and then issued a show cause notice on 12 th  August 2025 for Rs 414.88 Crores excluding interest and penalties. The notice alleges that services provided by Infosys's overseas branches are not export of services and that refunds claimed were erroneous. Infosys stated it assessed the merits, obtained external tax and legal advice, and filed a Writ Petition in the Hon'ble High Court of Karnataka on 19 th  September 2025 challenging the legitimacy of the notice. Infosys stated there is no tax demand as on date and no material impact from the article.

key_facts.fact: Media reports published on 8 th  October 2025 concerned a show cause notice issued by Directorate General of GST Intelligence for Rs 415 crore to Infosys over alleged ineligible ITC refunds.
DGGI sought information in May 2025 on GST refunds claimed by the Company, and Infosys provided relevant information and held meetings with DGGI officials.
DGGI issued a pre-show cause on 30 th  July 2025, and Infosys sought additional information, relevant documents, and time to respond.
DGGI issued a show cause notice on 12 th  August 2025 for Rs 414.88 Crores excluding interest and penalties.
The show cause notice alleges that services provided by the company's overseas branches are not export of services and that the refund claimed by the Company is allegedly erroneous.
Infosys filed a Writ Petition in the Hon'ble High Court of Karnataka on 19 th  September 2025 challenging the legitimacy of the show cause notice.
Infosys stated that there is no tax demand as on date against the Company regarding this matter.

litigation_or_regulatory_matters.description: DGGI issued a show cause notice alleging that services provided by Infosys's overseas branches are not export of services and that GST refunds claimed by the Company are allegedly erroneous.
litigation_or_regulatory_matters.amount: Rs 414.88 Crores excluding interest and penalties
litigation_or_regulatory_matters.status: The Company has on 19 th  September 2025, filed a Writ Petition in the Hon'ble High Court of Karnataka challenging the legitimacy of the show cause notice. There is no tax demand as on date against the Company regarding this matter.

risk_factors.risk: DGGI has challenged the GST refund treatment for services provided by Infosys's overseas branches, alleging they are not export of services.
risk_factors.potential_impact: The show cause notice amount is Rs 414.88 Crores excluding interest and penalties.

important_text_spans.text: DGGI did not provide the additional time requested and instead issued a show cause notice on 12 th  August 2025 for Rs 414.88 Crores excluding interest and penalties.
important_text_spans.reason: Supports the amount and timing of the regulatory tax notice.

important_text_spans.text: As per the show cause notice from DGGI, the services provided by the company's overseas branches are not export of services and hence the refund claimed by the Company is allegedly erroneous.
important_text_spans.reason: Supports the nature of DGGI's allegation.

important_text_spans.text: The Company has assessed the merits of the show cause notice and also sought advice from external tax consultants and legal counsels.
Pursuant to the advice, the Company has on 19 th  September 2025, filed a Writ Petition in the Hon'ble High Court of Karnataka challenging the legitimacy of the show cause notice.
important_text_spans.reason: Supports the current legal status of the dispute.

important_text_spans.text: There is no tax demand as on date against the Company regarding this matter.
important_text_spans.reason: Supports Infosys's stated position that no tax demand currently exists.

processing_notes: The media report amount is stated as Rs 415 crore, while the company-stated show cause notice amount is Rs 414.88 Crores excluding interest and penalties; both are preserved where relevant.
